name: collections-extended

metadata.name: collections-extended
classifiers: License :: OSI Approved :: Apache Software License
Topic :: Software Development :: Libraries :: Python Modules
metadata.summary: Extra Python Collections - bags (multisets) and setlists (ordered sets)

documentation:
# README

```{image} https://coveralls.io/repos/github/mlenzen/collections-extended/badge.svg?branch=master
:alt: Coverage
:target: https://coveralls.io/github/mlenzen/collections-extended?branch=master
```

```{image} https://pepy.tech/badge/collections-extended/week
:alt: Downloads
:target: https://pepy.tech/project/collections-extended/
```

Documentation:

: <http://collections-extended.lenzm.net/>

GitHub:

: <https://github.com/mlenzen/collections-extended>

PyPI:

: <https://pypi.python.org/pypi/collections-extended>

## Overview

`collections_extended` is a pure Python module with no dependencies providing:

- a `bag` class, AKA **multiset**,
- a `setlist` class, which is a **unique list** or **ordered set**,
- a `bijection` class, `RangeMap` which is a mapping from ranges to values and
- a `IndexedDict` class, which is an ordered mapping whose elements can be accessed using index, in addition to key.

There are also frozen (hashable) varieties of bags and setlists.

Compatible with and tested against Python 3.6, 3.7, 3.8, 3.9, 3.10 & PyPy3.

## Getting Started

```python
>>> from collections_extended import bag, setlist, bijection, RangeMap, IndexedDict
>>> from datetime import date
>>> b = bag('abracadabra')
>>> b.count('a')
5
>>> b.remove('a')
>>> b.count('a')
4
>>> 'a' in b
True
>>> b.count('d')
1
>>> b.remove('d')
>>> b.count('d')
0
>>> 'd' in b
False

>>> sl = setlist('abracadabra')
>>> sl
setlist(('a', 'b', 'r', 'c', 'd'))
>>> sl[3]
'c'
>>> sl[-1]
'd'
>>> 'r' in sl  # testing for inclusion is fast
True
>>> sl.index('d')  # so is finding the index of an element
4
>>> sl.insert(1, 'd')  # inserting an element already in raises a ValueError
Traceback (most recent call last):
...
        raise ValueError
ValueError
>>> sl.index('d')
4

>>> bij = bijection({'a': 1, 'b': 2, 'c': 3})
>>> bij.inverse[2]
'b'
>>> bij['a'] = 2
>>> bij == bijection({'a': 2, 'c': 3})
True
>>> bij.inverse[1] = 'a'
>>> bij == bijection({'a': 1, 'c': 3})
True

>>> version = RangeMap()
>>> version[date(2017, 10, 20): date(2017, 10, 27)] = '0.10.1'
>>> version[date(2017, 10, 27): date(2018, 2, 14)] = '1.0.0'
>>> version[date(2018, 2, 14):] = '1.0.1'
>>> version[date(2017, 10, 24)]
'0.10.1'
>>> version[date(2018, 7, 1)]
'1.0.1'
>>> version[date(2018, 6, 30):] = '1.0.2'
>>> version[date(2018, 7, 1)]
'1.0.2'

>>> idict = IndexedDict()
>>> idict['a'] = "A"
>>> idict['b'] = "B"
>>> idict['c'] = "C"
>>> idict.get(key='a')
'A'
>>> idict.get(index=2)
'C'
>>> idict.index('b')
1
```

## Installation

`pip install collections-extended`

## Usage

> `from collections_extended import bag, frozenbag, setlist, frozensetlist, bijection`

## Classes

There are seven new collections provided:

### Bags

bag

: This is a bag AKA multiset.

frozenbag

: This is a frozen (hashable) version of a bag.

### Setlists

setlist

: An ordered set or a list of unique elements depending on how you look at it.

frozensetlist

: This is a frozen (hashable) version of a setlist.

### Mappings

bijection

: A one-to-one mapping.

RangeMap

: A mapping from ranges (of numbers/dates/etc)

IndexedDict

: A mapping that keeps insertion order and allows access by index.

### Python 2

The package no longer supports Python 2. The last version to support
Python 2.7, 3.4 & 3.5 was 1.0. No new feature releases will be done for 1.x but
any significant bugs that come up may be fixed.


modules.name: collections_extended._util
documentation: util functions for collections_extended.

functions.name: collections_extended._util.hash_iterable
documentation:
Perform a O(1) memory hash of an iterable of arbitrary length.

hash(tuple(it)) creates a temporary tuple containing all values from it
which could be a problem if it is large.

See discussion at:
https://groups.google.com/forum/#!msg/python-ideas/XcuC01a8SYs/e-doB9TbDwAJ

Represents a subrange of a RangeMap.

This is a glorified namedtuple.

classes.name: collections_extended.range_map.RangeMapView

methods.name: collections_extended.range_map.RangeMapView.__init__
documentation: Create a RangeMapView from a RangeMap.

methods.name: collections_extended.range_map.RangeMapView.mapping
documentation: Return the underlying RangeMap.
Base class for views of RangeMaps.

classes.name: collections_extended.range_map.RangeMapKeysView
documentation:
A view of the keys that mark the starts of subranges of a RangeMap.

Since iterating over all the keys is impossible, the view only
iterates over the keys that start each subrange.

classes.name: collections_extended.range_map.RangeMapItemsView
documentation:
A view of the items that mark the starts of subranges of a RangeMap.

Since iterating over all the items is impossible, the view only
iterates over the items that start each subrange.

classes.name: collections_extended.range_map.RangeMapValuesView
documentation:
A view on the values that mark the start of subranges of a RangeMap.

Since iterating over all the values is impossible, the view only
iterates over the values that start each subrange.

classes.name: collections_extended.range_map.RangeMap

methods.name: collections_extended.range_map.RangeMap.__init__
documentation:
Create a RangeMap.

A mapping or other iterable can be passed to initialize the RangeMap.
If mapping is passed, it is interpreted as a mapping from range start
indices to values.
If an iterable is passed, each element will define a range in the
RangeMap and should be formatted (start, stop, value).

default_value is a an optional keyword argument that will initialize the
entire RangeMap to that value. Any missing ranges will be mapped to that
value. However, if ranges are subsequently deleted they will be removed
and *not* mapped to the default_value.

Args:
        iterable: A Mapping or an Iterable to initialize from.
        default_value: If passed, the return value for all keys less than the
                least key in mapping or missing ranges in iterable. If no mapping
                or iterable, the return value for all keys.

methods.name: collections_extended.range_map.RangeMap.from_mapping
documentation: Create a RangeMap from a mapping of interval starts to values.

methods.name: collections_extended.range_map.RangeMap.from_iterable
documentation:
Create a RangeMap from an iterable of tuples defining each range.

Each element of the iterable is a tuple (start, stop, value).

methods.name: collections_extended.range_map.RangeMap.ranges
documentation:
Generate MappedRanges for all mapped ranges.

Yields:
        MappedRange

methods.name: collections_extended.range_map.RangeMap._getitem

documentation: Get the value of the range containing key, otherwise return restval.

methods.name: collections_extended.range_map.RangeMap.get_range

methods.name: collections_extended.range_map.RangeMap.empty

Like delete, but no Error is raised if the entire range isn't mapped.

methods.name: collections_extended.range_map.RangeMap.end

None if RangeMap is empty or unbounded to the right.

classes.name: collections_extended.indexed_dict.IndexedDict

methods.name: collections_extended.indexed_dict.IndexedDict.__init__
documentation: Create an IndexedDict and initialize it like a dict.

methods.name: collections_extended.indexed_dict.IndexedDict.clear
documentation: Remove all items.

methods.name: collections_extended.indexed_dict.IndexedDict.get
documentation:
Return value with given `key` or `index`.

If no value is found, return `default` (`None` by default).

:::{deprecated} 1.0.3
:::

The `d` parameter has been renamed `default`. `d` will be removed in
some future version.

Args:

: key: The key of the value to get
  index: The index of the value to get
  default: The value to return if `key` is not found or `index` is

  > out of bounds. If it is NOT_SET, None is returned.

  d: DEPRECATED: Old parameter name for `default`

methods.name: collections_extended.indexed_dict.IndexedDict.pop
documentation:
Remove and return value.

Optionally, specify the `key` or `index` of the value to pop.
If `key` is specified and is not found a `KeyError` is raised unless
`default` is specified. Likewise, if `index` is specified that is out of
bounds, an `IndexError` is raised unless `default` is specified.

Both `index` and `key` cannot be specified. If neither is specified,
then the last value is popped.

This is generally O(N) unless removing last item, then O(1).

:::{deprecated} 1.0.3
:::

The `d` parameter has been renamed `default`. `d` will be removed in
some future version.

Args:

: key: The key of the value to pop
  index: The index of the value to pop
  default: The value to return if the key is not found or the index is

  > out of bounds

  d: DEPRECATED: Old parameter name for `default`

methods.name: collections_extended.indexed_dict.IndexedDict._pop_key
documentation: Remove an element by key.

methods.name: collections_extended.indexed_dict.IndexedDict._pop_index
documentation: Remove an element by index, or last element.

methods.name: collections_extended.indexed_dict.IndexedDict.fast_pop
documentation:
Pop a specific item quickly by swapping it to the end.

Remove value with given key or index (last item by default) fast
by swapping it to the last place first.

Changes order of the remaining items (item that used to be last goes to
the popped location).
Returns tuple of (poped_value, new_moved_index, moved_key, moved_value).
If key is not found raises KeyError or IndexError.

Runs in O(1).

methods.name: collections_extended.indexed_dict.IndexedDict.popitem
documentation:
Remove and return a (key, value) tuple.

By default, the last item is popped.
Optionally, specify the `key` or `index` of the value to pop.
The `last` parameter is included to match the OrderedDict API. If `last`
is passed then the first or last item is returned based on its
truthiness.

At most one of `index`, `last` and `key` can be specified.

This is generally O(N) unless removing last item, then O(1).

Args:
        key: The key of the value to pop
        index: The index of the value to pop
        last: Whether or not to pip the last item

Raises:
        KeyError: If the dictionary is empty or a key is specified that is
                not present
        IndexError: If `index` is specified and is out of bounds
        ValueError: If more than one of `last`, `index` and `key` are
                specified

methods.name: collections_extended.indexed_dict.IndexedDict.move_to_end
documentation:
Move an existing element to the end (or beginning if last==False).

Runs in O(N).

methods.name: collections_extended.indexed_dict.IndexedDict.copy
documentation: Return a shallow copy.

methods.name: collections_extended.indexed_dict.IndexedDict.index
documentation:
Return index of a record with given key.

Runs in O(1).

methods.name: collections_extended.indexed_dict.IndexedDict.key
documentation:
Return key of a record at given index.

Runs in O(1).

methods.name: collections_extended.indexed_dict.IndexedDict.__len__
documentation: Return number of elements stored.

methods.name: collections_extended.indexed_dict.IndexedDict.__getitem__
documentation:
Return value corresponding to given key.

Raises KeyError when the key is not present in the mapping.

Runs in O(1).

methods.name: collections_extended.indexed_dict.IndexedDict.__setitem__
documentation:
Set item with given key to given value.

If the key is already present in the mapping its order is unchanged,
if it is not then it's added to the last place.

Runs in O(1).

methods.name: collections_extended.indexed_dict.IndexedDict.__delitem__
documentation:
Remove item with given key from the mapping.

Runs in O(n), unless removing last item, then in O(1).

methods.name: collections_extended.indexed_dict.IndexedDict.__contains__
documentation:
Check if a key is present in the mapping.

Runs in O(1).

methods.name: collections_extended.indexed_dict.IndexedDict.__iter__
documentation: Return iterator over the keys of the mapping in order.

A Mapping that preserves insertion order and allows access by item index.

The API is an extension of OrderedDict.

A view for the unique items and their counts in a bag.

A view for the unique items and their counts in a bag.

classes.name: collections_extended.bags.Bag

methods.name: collections_extended.bags.Bag.__init__
documentation:
Create a new bag.

If iterable isn't given, is None or is empty then the bag starts empty.
Otherwise each element from iterable will be added to the bag
however many times it appears.

This runs in O(len(iterable))
Basic object methods

methods.name: collections_extended.bags.Bag.copy
documentation:
Create a shallow copy of self.

This runs in O(len(self.num_unique_elements()))

methods.name: collections_extended.bags.Bag.num_unique_elements
documentation:
Return the number of unique elements.

This runs in O(1) time
New public methods (not overriding/implementing anything)

methods.name: collections_extended.bags.Bag.unique_elements
documentation: Return a view of unique elements in this bag.

methods.name: collections_extended.bags.Bag.count
documentation:
Return the number of value present in this bag.

If value is not in the bag no Error is raised, instead 0 is returned.

This runs in O(1) time

Args:
        value: The element of self to get the count of
Returns:
        int: The count of value in self

methods.name: collections_extended.bags.Bag.nlargest
documentation:
List the n most common elements and their counts.

List is from the most
common to the least.  If n is None, the list all element counts.

Run time should be O(m log m) where m is len(self)
Args:
        n (int): The number of elements to return

methods.name: collections_extended.bags.Bag.counts
documentation:
Return a view of the unique elements in self and their counts.

:::{versionadded} 1.0.3
:::

methods.name: collections_extended.bags.Bag.from_mapping
documentation:
Create a bag from a dict of elem->count.

Each key in the dict is added if the value is > 0.

Raises:
        ValueError: If any count is < 0.

methods.name: collections_extended.bags.Bag.__len__
documentation:
Return the cardinality of the bag.

This runs in O(1)
implementing Sized methods

methods.name: collections_extended.bags.Bag.__contains__
documentation:
Return the multiplicity of the element.

This runs in O(1)
implementing Container methods

methods.name: collections_extended.bags.Bag.__iter__
documentation:
Iterate through all elements.

Multiple copies will be returned if they exist.
implementing Iterable methods

methods.name: collections_extended.bags.Bag.issubset
documentation:
Check that every element in self has a count <= in other.

Args:
        other (Iterable)
Comparison methods

methods.name: collections_extended.bags.Bag.issuperset
documentation:
Check that every element in self has a count >= in other.

Args:
        other (Iterable)

methods.name: collections_extended.bags.Bag._iadd
documentation: Operations - &, |, +, -, ^, * and isdisjoint

methods.name: collections_extended.bags.Bag._iand
collections_extended.bags.Bag._ior
collections_extended.bags.Bag._ixor
collections_extended.bags.Bag._isub
collections_extended.bags.Bag.__and__
documentation:
Intersection is the minimum of corresponding counts.

This runs in O(l + n) where:
        * n is self.num_unique_elements()
        * `l = 1` if other is a bag else `l = len(other)`

methods.name: collections_extended.bags.Bag.isdisjoint
documentation:
Return if this bag is disjoint with the passed collection.

This runs in O(len(other))

methods.name: collections_extended.bags.Bag.__or__
documentation:
Union is the maximum of all elements.

This runs in O(m + n) where:
        * `n = self.num_unique_elements()`
        * m = other.num_unique_elements() if other is a bag else m = len(other)

methods.name: collections_extended.bags.Bag.__add__
documentation:
Return a new bag also containing all the elements of other.

self + other = self & other + self | other

This runs in O(m + n) where:
        * n is self.num_unique_elements()
        * m is len(other)

Args:
        other (Iterable): elements to add to self

methods.name: collections_extended.bags.Bag.__sub__
documentation:
Difference between the sets.

For normal sets this is all x s.t. x in self and x not in other.
For bags this is count(x) = max(0, self.count(x)-other.count(x))

This runs in O(m + n) where:
        * n is self.num_unique_elements()
        * m is len(other)

Args:
        other (Iterable): elements to remove

methods.name: collections_extended.bags.Bag.__mul__
documentation: Cartesian product with other.

methods.name: collections_extended.bags.Bag.product
documentation:
Cartesian product of the two sets.

Optionally, pass an operator to combine elements instead of creating a
tuple.

This should run in O(m*n+l) where:
        * `m` is the number of unique elements in `self`
        * `n` is the number of unique elements in `other`
        * `l` is 0 if `other` is a bag, else `l` is the `len(other)`

Args:
        other (Iterable): The iterable to take the product with.
        operator (Callable): A function that accepts an element from self
                and other and returns a combined value to include in the return
                value.

methods.name: collections_extended.bags.Bag.__xor__
documentation:
Symmetric difference between the sets.

other can be any iterable.

This runs in O(m + n) where:
        m = len(self)
        n = len(other)

Base class for bag classes.

Base class for bag and frozenbag. Is not mutable and not hashable, so there's
no reason to use this instead of either bag or frozenbag.

modules.name: collections_extended.setlists
documentation: Setlist class definitions.

classes.name: collections_extended.setlists.SetList

methods.name: collections_extended.setlists.SetList.__init__
documentation:
Create a setlist, initializing from iterable if present.

Args:
        iterable (Iterable): Values to initialize the setlist with.
        raise_on_duplicate: Raise a ValueError if any duplicate values
                are present.

methods.name: collections_extended.setlists.SetList._fix_neg_index
documentation: Convenience methods

methods.name: collections_extended.setlists.SetList.__contains__
documentation: Implement Container

methods.name: collections_extended.setlists.SetList.__len__
documentation: Iterable we get by inheriting from Sequence
Implement Sized

methods.name: collections_extended.setlists.SetList.__getitem__
documentation: Implement Sequence

methods.name: collections_extended.setlists.SetList.count
documentation:
Return the number of occurrences of value in self.

This runs in O(1)

Args:
        value: The value to count
Returns:
        int: 1 if the value is in the setlist, otherwise 0

methods.name: collections_extended.setlists.SetList.index
documentation:
Return the index of value between start and end.

By default, the entire setlist is searched.

This runs in O(1)

Args:
        value: The value to find the index of
        start (int): The index to start searching at (defaults to 0)
        end (int): The index to stop searching at (defaults to the end of the list)
Returns:
        int: The index of the value
Raises:
        ValueError: If the value is not in the list or outside of start - end
        IndexError: If start or end are out of range

methods.name: collections_extended.setlists.SetList.issubset
documentation: Report whether another set contains this set.
Implement Set

methods.name: collections_extended.setlists.SetList.issuperset
documentation: Report whether this set contains another set.

methods.name: collections_extended.setlists.SetList.union
documentation:
Return the union of sets as a new set.

(i.e. all elements that are in either set.)

methods.name: collections_extended.setlists.SetList.intersection
documentation:
Return the intersection of two sets as a new set.

(i.e. all elements that are in both sets.)

methods.name: collections_extended.setlists.SetList.difference
documentation:
Return the difference of two or more sets as a new set.

(i.e. all elements that are in this set but not the others.)

methods.name: collections_extended.setlists.SetList.symmetric_difference
documentation:
Return the symmetric difference (disjuntive union) of two sets.

(i.e. all elements that are in one set but not both.)

methods.name: collections_extended.setlists.SetList.__eq__
documentation: Comparison

methods.name: collections_extended.setlists.SetList.sub_index
documentation:
Return the index of a subsequence.

This runs in O(len(sub))

Args:
        sub (Sequence): An Iterable to search for
        start (int): The index at which to start the search
        end (int): The index at which to end the search
Returns:
        int: The index of the first element of sub
Raises:
        ValueError: If sub isn't a subsequence
        TypeError: If sub isn't iterable
        IndexError: If start or end are out of range
New methods

methods.name: collections_extended.setlists.SetList.copy
documentation: Return a shallow copy of the setlist.

A setlist is an ordered `Collection` of unique elements.

`SetList` is the superclass of `setlist` and `frozensetlist`. It is
immutable and unhashable.

classes.name: collections_extended.setlists.setlist

methods.name: collections_extended.setlists.setlist._delete_all
documentation: Helper methods

methods.name: collections_extended.setlists.setlist.pop
documentation: Remove and return the item at index.
Set/Sequence agnostic

methods.name: collections_extended.setlists.setlist.clear
documentation: Remove all elements from self.

methods.name: collections_extended.setlists.setlist.__setitem__
documentation: Implement MutableSequence

methods.name: collections_extended.setlists.setlist.insert
documentation:
Insert value at index.

Args:
        index (int): Index to insert value at
        value: Value to insert
Raises:
        ValueError: If value already in self
        IndexError: If start or end are out of range

methods.name: collections_extended.setlists.setlist.append
documentation:
Append value to the end.

Args:
        value: Value to append
Raises:
        ValueError: If value alread in self
        TypeError: If value isn't hashable

methods.name: collections_extended.setlists.setlist.extend
documentation:
Append all values to the end.

If any of the values are present, ValueError will
be raised and none of the values will be appended.

Args:
        values (Iterable): Values to append
Raises:
        ValueError: If any values are already present or there are duplicates
                in the passed values.
        TypeError: If any of the values aren't hashable.

methods.name: collections_extended.setlists.setlist.__iadd__
documentation:
Add all values to the end of self.

Args:
        values (Iterable): Values to append
Raises:
        ValueError: If any values are already present

methods.name: collections_extended.setlists.setlist.remove
documentation:
Remove value from self.

Args:
        value: Element to remove from self
Raises:
        ValueError: if element is already present

methods.name: collections_extended.setlists.setlist.remove_all
documentation:
Remove all elements from elems_to_delete, raises ValueErrors.

See Also:
        discard_all
Args:
        elems_to_delete (Iterable): Elements to remove.
Raises:
        ValueError: If the count of any element is greater in
                elems_to_delete than self.
        TypeError: If any of the values aren't hashable.

methods.name: collections_extended.setlists.setlist.reverse
documentation: Reverse the setlist in-place.

methods.name: collections_extended.setlists.setlist.add
documentation:
Add an item.

Note:
        This does not raise a ValueError for an already present value like
        append does. This is to match the behavior of set.add
Args:
        item: Item to add
Raises:
        TypeError: If item isn't hashable.
Implement MutableSet

methods.name: collections_extended.setlists.setlist.update
documentation:
Add all values to the end.

If any of the values are present, silently ignore
them (as opposed to extend which raises an Error).

See also:
        extend
Args:
        values (Iterable): Values to add
Raises:
        TypeError: If any of the values are unhashable.

methods.name: collections_extended.setlists.setlist.discard_all
documentation:
Discard all the elements from elems_to_delete.

This is much faster than removing them one by one.
This runs in O(len(self) + len(elems_to_delete))

Args:
        elems_to_delete (Iterable): Elements to discard.
Raises:
        TypeError: If any of the values aren't hashable.

methods.name: collections_extended.setlists.setlist.discard
documentation:
Discard an item.

Note:
        This does not raise a ValueError for a missing value like remove does.
        This matches the behavior of set.discard

methods.name: collections_extended.setlists.setlist.difference_update
documentation: Update self to include only the difference with other.

methods.name: collections_extended.setlists.setlist.intersection_update
documentation: Update self to include only the intersection with other.

methods.name: collections_extended.setlists.setlist.symmetric_difference_update
documentation: Update self to include only the symmetric difference with other.

methods.name: collections_extended.setlists.setlist.shuffle
documentation:
Shuffle all of the elements in self in place.

Args:
        random: A function returning a random float in [0.0, 1.0). If none
                is passed, the default from `random.shuffle` will be used.
New methods

methods.name: collections_extended.setlists.setlist.sort
documentation: Sort this setlist in place.

methods.name: collections_extended.setlists.setlist.swap
documentation:
Swap the values at indices i & j.
